role: First Deputy Speaker of Parliament, 6th Majlis (alternating 2000–2004); Co-founder, OMIIR
bio: Behzad Nabavi is a senior figure of the Islamic left turned democratic reformist. An electrical engineer, he fought against the Shah and was imprisoned before the revolution. He co-founded the Islamic Revolution Committees (komiteh) and a forerunner to the Ministry of Intelligence. Under [PM Mousavi](/people/mir-hossein-mousavi) he served as minister for executive affairs and minister of heavy industry. He was also Iran's chief negotiator in the 1981 [Algiers Accords](/events/algiers-accords-1981) that ended the hostage crisis. In the 6th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) he alternated as First Deputy Speaker with [Mohammad-Reza Khatami](/people/mohammad-reza-khatami). He was arrested after the 2009 elections and sentenced to six years.
editorial_notes: Born 1941 in Tehran. Iran's chief negotiator in Algiers Accords (1981). Arrested post-2009, sentenced 6 years. Co-founder of Ministry of Intelligence's predecessor body.
